response:
Section 1  
I need to write a Python function that counts the number of occurrences of a specific word in a given text, ignoring case sensitivity. The function should return an integer representing how many times the specified word appears in the text.

Section 2  
The function will be called `count_word_occurrences` and will take two parameters: `text` and `word`. The function will process the text to count the occurrences of the word, ignoring case sensitivity.

Section 3  
To handle case insensitivity, I will convert both the `text` and `word` to lowercase (or uppercase) before comparing. This ensures that words like "Freedom" and "freedom" are considered the same.

Section 4  
I will use the `count` method of the string to find the occurrences of the word in the text. However, since the `count` method is case-sensitive, I will first convert both the `text` and `word` to lowercase (or uppercase) to make the comparison case-insensitive.

P.S.: Your response must have 4 sections.
